feat: harden non-destructive guards and config validation (#38) (#42)

- Expand BLOCKED_SUBCOMMANDS with 14 additional mutating commands:
  switch, restore, cherry-pick, revert, pull, apply, am, init,
  clone, gc, prune, bisect, filter-branch, update-ref

- Expand BLOCKED_ARG_SEQUENCES with 25 additional destructive
  sequences for mixed-mode commands: branch rename/copy/delete,
  remote add/remove/rename/set-url, worktree add/remove/prune,
  submodule add/init/update/deinit, notes add/remove/edit/merge/prune,
  reflog delete/expire

- Add .strict() to all Zod config schemas to reject unknown properties
  matching schema.json additionalProperties:false intent

- Enforce allowExtraArgs/extraArgsSchema consistency:
  allowExtraArgs=true requires extraArgsSchema,
  allowExtraArgs=false forbids extraArgsSchema

- Validate default values are compatible with enum constraints
  at config load time

- Add 80 new unit tests covering all additions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix content-level citation accuracy: 26 line numbers + 1 default value

Flywheel rotation 6: Content-level citation verification
Checked 292 (identifier, file:line) claims — verified actual GAMS file
content matches the cited line number.

Fixes:
- 24 wrong line numbers across 9 module docs (mostly off by 1-5 lines
  due to code evolution since docs were written)
- 1 wrong variable prefix in module_20.md (i20_ vs f20_ at input.gms)
- 1 wrong default value in module_56.md (s56_cprice_red_factor default
  is 1, not 0)
- 1 citation line fix in module_32.md (s32_aff_prot location)

Also excluded 2 false positives (conceptually correct references where
the identifier name doesn't literally appear at the cited line).

Result: 266/292 content-verified claims (91%) → 290/292 (99.3%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: enable implemented features in Test262 skip list (#382)

Remove 40+ feature tags from UNSUPPORTED_FEATURES that have been
implemented: async-functions, generators, classes, symbols, BigInt,
destructuring, for-of, dynamic-import, Proxy/Reflect, WeakRef,
FinalizationRegistry, Intl, regexp-dotall/lookbehind/named-groups/
match-indices, tail-call-optimization, globalThis, import.meta,
export-star-as-namespace-from-module.

Remaining skip list: top-level-await, Atomics/SharedArrayBuffer,
regexp-unicode-property-escapes, regexp-v-flag, Temporal,
TypedArray/ArrayBuffer/DataView, ShadowRealm,
explicit-resource-management, import-assertions/attributes,
arbitrary-module-namespace-names.

CI threshold raised from 10% to 25%.

commit.message:
refactor: reduce complexity in 9 critical functions (55-83% reduction)

Refactored 9 functions exceeding professional complexity thresholds:
- validateDynEntries (elf): 17.9 → 3.1 (82.7% ↓)
- Unload (loader): 16.3 → 3.1 (81.0% ↓)
- mapSegments (loader): 16.3 → 4.9 (69.9% ↓)
- LoadFromDynamic (symbol): 15.8 → 6.2 (60.8% ↓)
- findLibrary (dl): 15.3 → 4.4 (71.2% ↓)
- collectProgramHeaders (elf): 15.0 → 6.7 (55.3% ↓)
- finalizeObject (loader): 15.0 → 3.1 (79.3% ↓)
- ResolveWithLibrary (dl): 15.0 → 3.1 (79.3% ↓)
- Resolve (dl): 15.0 → 4.4 (70.7% ↓)

Extracted 31 focused helper functions following project conventions:
- Verb-first naming (validateAddressTag, searchInDirs, etc.)
- Private (lowercase) functions for internal logic
- Each helper ≤20 lines, cyclomatic ≤8
- GoDoc comments matching project style

Quality improvements:
- Average cyclomatic complexity: 10.7 → 2.9 (73% ↓)
- Average overall complexity: 15.7 → 4.5 (71% ↓)
- Average function length: 50.2 → 11.4 lines (77% ↓)
- All functions now meet professional thresholds (<9.0)

Testing: All existing tests pass (go test ./...)
No behavioral changes - pure extract-method refactoring

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs/chore: changelog phase restructure and proof artifact hygiene (#173)

* fix(hygiene): update root hygiene policy to include missing allowed paths

* feat(extractor): add phase s registry and audit scaffolding

* fix(extractor): harden d-phase routing retries

* fix(extractor): harden phase d reliability

* feat(repo-truth-extractor): implement TP-008 json fill+repair lanes

* chore(repo-truth-extractor): log contract operations

* fix(run_extraction_v3): correct strict route indentation

* style(run_extraction_v3): apply black formatting

* feat(repo-truth-extractor): add TP-008 audit tool

* fix(repo-truth-extractor): restore promptset model map source

* feat(repo-truth-extractor): split classic v3 and add v5 telemetry UX

* feat(extractor): v5 transition + absolute 100% coverage + safety mandates

- Systematically patched all prompt source scopes for absolute 100% repo coverage.
- Integrated modern architectural surfaces: Hooks, Agents, and Editor Configs (Vibe/Copilot).
- Registered 6 new integration steps and 7 artifacts in promptset/artifacts registries.
- Authored 6 new v5-style prompts for integration surface mapping.
- Added 'sync_prompt_scopes.py' tool and integrated '--sync' flag in runner.
- Codified 'NO LIVE RUNNER' safety mandate in GEMINI.md to prevent credit consumption.
- Fixed UnboundLocalError and registry referential integrity (PROMPTSET_INVALID).

* refactor(cli): extract 13 inline @cli.group() definitions into commands/ submodules

Split the monolithic cli.py (7402 lines) into focused command modules under
src/dopemux/commands/. Each group now lives in its own file with correct
relative imports (..module). cli.py reduced to ~4606 lines and uses
cli.add_command() for all groups.

New modules: autoresponder, capture_group, code, decisions, dev, extract,
extractor, memory, profile, trigger_group, update, upgrades, workflow_group.

Also: add Serena ~/.serena and host code volume mounts to compose.yml,
remove stale docker/mcp-servers-source/docker-compose.yml duplicate,
update test monkeypatches to target capture/trigger module namespaces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): move autoresponder config subcommand into autoresponder_commands module

The config subcommand was left in cli.py after extraction, causing a
NameError for _get_autoresponder_config_manager which now lives in the
commands module. Move the command definition to the correct file.

* docs(changelog): update unreleased section with Phase A release notes

commit.message:
fix: show all marketplaces (registered + cache-only), enable delete for both (#118)

## Root cause

\config.json\ only has \office-coding-agent\ registered. All other cache
dirs (\mcaps-microsoft--SPT-IQ\, \stbrnner_microsoft--SPT-IQ\,
\gency-*\) were cloned by the CLI during browsing but never registered.
v0.5.15 accidentally hid them entirely.

## Changes

- **\listMarketplaces()\**: Returns registered entries AND cache-only
dirs (registeredKey=null)
- **\ emoveMarketplace(cacheDir, slug, registeredKey)\**: Registered →
CLI unregister + rmSync cache dir; cache-only → rmSync only
- **Delete button**: Visible for all \!isBuiltIn && !isOwn\ entries — no
\ egisteredKey\ guard
- **\pluginService.ts\**: \postJson\ body type widened to allow \
ull\ values
- **Tests**: 28 tests — cache-only entries ARE listed; cache-only delete
works; delete button shown when \ egisteredKey\ is null

Co-authored-by: Stefan Broenner <stefan.broenner@microsoft.comm>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(api): update package_id on user profile when subscription plan changes

The subscription-updated webhook processor was updating paddle_price_id
on indb_payment_subscriptions but never resolving which package it
belonged to. The user's indb_auth_user_profiles.package_id stayed stale,
so the frontend still showed the old plan after upgrade/downgrade.

Now resolves the new package by scanning pricing_tiers for the matching
paddle_price_id and updates both indb_payment_subscriptions.package_id
and indb_auth_user_profiles.package_id.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ac): auto-resume only most recent interrupted session per project [skip deploy]

Prevents relaunching all interrupted AC sessions on restart (which creates
multiple concurrent cycles). Now uses DISTINCT ON project_id to resume
only the latest session per project, with SQLite GROUP BY fallback.

commit.message:
Add interactive Governance Scorecard assessment tool

Adapt FSI-AgentGov's 6-phase interactive assessment for CopilotGov:
- 54 controls across 4 pillars (Readiness, Security, Compliance, Operations)
- Cumulative governance level model (Baseline/Recommended/Regulated)
  replaces AgentGov's zone-based model
- Inverted risk priority: baseline gaps weighted highest (most urgent)
- Multi-level score display with per-pillar breakdown
- Full export capabilities (JSON, Excel, CSV, print-to-PDF)
- All data stays in browser (localStorage), zero server dependencies

New files:
- docs/assessment/index.md - Scorecard page with SPA container
- docs/javascripts/assessment-app.js - Main SPA (~95KB)
- docs/javascripts/assessment-loader.js - Lazy loader
- docs/javascripts/assessment-data.json - Generated control data
- docs/javascripts/lib/chart.min.js - Vendored Chart.js 4.4.7
- docs/stylesheets/assessment.css - Assessment styles with dark mode
- scripts/extract_assessment_data.py - Data extraction from controls

Updated files:
- mkdocs.yml - Added extra_javascript, nav entry, exclude_docs
- .github/workflows/publish_docs.yml - Added data generation step
- .gitignore - Exclude generated assessment-data.json
- docs/start-here.md - Added scorecard scenario and tip callout
- docs/index.md - Added scorecard to Quick Navigation

All CI checks pass: verify_language_rules.py (0 violations),
verify_controls.py (0 errors), mkdocs build --strict (clean).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  results from Min(top, idMap.Count)

  batch IDs list and HashSet from JSON array length (capped at 500)
- BinaryApiHandlers: size entity lists via ICollection check, extract
  aggregation query result for capacity check
- RouteHandlers: size CSV result lists via ICollection check

fix: auto-reconnect to healthy peer when active masternode goes down

Every 5s peer discovery re-probes all peers. Previously the active
client was only set on first connection (client.is_none()). Now, if
the active peer is no longer healthy in the latest probe results the
wallet immediately switches to the best available healthy peer,
restarts the WS connection, and refreshes balance data.

commit.message:
fix: clip filter output to filter region to prevent feFlood bleeding (#131)

Filter primitives like feFlood fill the entire sourceGraphic canvas,
causing each filtered element's output to overwrite previous elements
when composited. Fix by clipping the filter result to the SVG filter
region (element bbox + 10% default, or explicit filter attributes)
before compositing back onto the main canvas.

Also supports filterUnits='userSpaceOnUse' and explicit x/y/width/height
on the <filter> element for custom filter regions.

Fixes #130

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: END_SESSION routing + OTPK soft-delete

END_SESSION routing (messaging-service):
- In GetPendingMessages and convert_kafka_envelope_to_proto, "END_SESSION"
  payload was falling through to ContentType::E2eeSignal — client tried to
  decrypt it as a Double Ratchet message and failed silently
- Map "END_SESSION" → ContentType::SessionReset (same as SESSION_RESET)
- Send empty encrypted_payload for all SessionReset/KeySync control messages
  so client cannot accidentally pass the ASCII type string to decryption

OTPK soft-delete (key-service + migration 027):
- replace_existing=true previously hard-deleted all OTPKs immediately
- Now marks them is_expired=true with expired_at=NOW() (soft-delete)
- GetPreKeyBundle queries skip expired keys (AND is_expired = false)
- get_prekey_count only counts active keys
- New cleanup_expired_otpks() hard-deletes after 48-hour TTL (same pattern
  as signed_prekey_archive)
- Migration 027 adds is_expired/expired_at columns + partial indexes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Francois Chastanet <francois.chastanet@crossknowledge.com>

Move form registry to Azure Blob Storage

Linux Consumption plan filesystem is read-only (squashfs) so
register-form couldn't persist new entries. Registry now
reads/writes from Azure Blob Storage using managed identity.

- config.py: load_registry_data/save_registry_data backed by blob
- Falls back to local file for development (USE_LOCAL_REGISTRY env)
- register_form + activate_form handlers use new API
- Removed FORM_REGISTRY_PATH from Bicep (no longer needed)
- Seeded blob with initial registry via az storage blob upload
- Updated all tests to mock blob-backed functions
- 80 tests passing, Bicep validated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix GL context NULL on Xvfb/Mesa: add XSync before make_current() (#361)

* Initial plan

* Fix GL context NULL on Xvfb/Mesa: add XSync before make_current()

On Xvfb with Mesa's software renderer (llvmpipe), glXMakeCurrent returns
True but glGetString(GL_VERSION) returns NULL because Fl::check() processes
FLTK events but does NOT call XSync.  Without XSync, the X server's
XMapWindow command (issued internally by show()) is still pending when
make_current() calls glXMakeCurrent.  Mesa accepts the binding (returns
True) but cannot allocate the back buffer for an unmapped window, leaving
GL non-functional.

Fix: add XSync(fl_display, False) after show()+Fl::check() and before
make_current() in two places:
- FLTKContextManager::ensureWindow() in fltk_context_manager.h
- ObolViewerWindow::primeGLContexts() in obol_viewer.cpp

This mirrors the pattern in headless_utils.h which calls
XMapWindow -> XSync(dpy, False) before glXCreateContext, and which
reliably initialises GL on Xvfb/Mesa in CI.

Verified with OBOL_GL_DIAG=1: GL_VERSION now shows
"4.5 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 25.2.8" at every diagnostic checkpoint
instead of NULL.  All 198 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: starseeker <238416+starseeker@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: starseeker <238416+starseeker@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(css): comprehensive light mode fixes — code bg, red accents, terminal chrome, clipboard button

- Add full CSS variable set to recallm-light scheme (bg, fg, code colors)
  so code blocks use light background (#f0f0f0) instead of inheriting dark vars
- Red accent text (.hero-eyebrow, .section-label, .step-number) now stays
  crimson (#C62828) in light mode via explicit !important overrides
- Hero terminal chrome (.hero-code-panel, .hero-code-titlebar) adapts to
  light mode with neutral gray palette instead of hardcoded dark colors
- .md-clipboard styled to match pip-install button: subtle border, crimson
  hover accent in both dark and light modes
- Hero install button, subtitle, and grid border adapted for light mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: pure-go-dl
repository.description: Pure go dynamic linker

commit.message:
fix(firmware): fix undefined behavior in I2C reads and eliminate heap fragmentation

- Fix Wire.read() chaining undefined evaluation order: read into separate
  uint8_t temporaries before combining into int16_t values
- Replace String() heap allocations with stack-based dtostrf() + char buffer
  to prevent SRAM fragmentation on Arduino Uno (2KB SRAM)
- No hardware pin assignments modified

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
